evidence.physics_rationale: Pushrod move repositions the front static platform while preserving legal garage correlation.
Rear pushrod move changes static rake support and rear platform stability.
Front heave rate is driven by travel margin, clean bottoming, and aero-platform control.
Front heave perch sets the static slider position and available heave travel.
Rear heave support is a tradeoff between rear platform control and exit traction compliance.
Front camber is tied to contact patch shape, carcass temperature, and understeer support.
Brake bias is adjusted from braking lock evidence and braking-platform stability.
Diff preload is set from exit traction demand and rotation stability.
TC gain follows measured rear power-slip demand.
Brake bias target is passed through from hardware/session context because the solver only solves static brake bias.
Brake migration is carried forward as hardware context; no migration model is currently solved by the pipeline.
Front master cylinder sizing is treated as pass-through hardware context rather than a solved setup output.
Rear master cylinder sizing is treated as pass-through hardware context rather than a solved setup output.
Pad compound is reported honestly as pass-through brake hardware context, not as a solved telemetry output.

solver_notes: Applied rematerialized compromise candidate result to final report/JSON/export payloads.

constraints.name: Roll spring rate in range (180 N/mm)
RARB sensitivity within useful range
constraints.note: <0.005 = insensitive (wrong bar size), >0.05 = too sensitive (step down)

car_specific_notes: Stiffer RARB -> more load transfer to rear -> front gains grip via LLTD. Use RARB as the primary live balance variable.
Cold tyre out-lap: softer RARB to prevent snap oversteer.

constraints.name: Front dynamic camber at peak g
Rear dynamic camber at peak g
Front toe within reasonable range
constraints.note: Excessive toe-out increases tyre scrub and lap time

constraints.name: Rear toe non-destabilizing
constraints.note: Rear toe-out is destabilizing — keep at or above 0mm

constraints.name: Front conditioning to op temp
constraints.note: Vision tread: >20 laps indicates tyres may not reach temp in a stint

notes: Representative cornering: 3.87g (p95=2.10g, peak=4.63g, kerb_weight=0.7). Roll: 4.0° at representative, 4.8° at peak.
Tyre temperature spread diagnosis: inner hotter = correct camber. If outer runs hotter -> reduce negative camber by 0.2-0.3 deg.
Porsche 963: target tyre temp 85-105°C (Michelin GTP compound). Conditioning typically 10-15 laps from cold start.
For sprint qualifying: add 0.2-0.3° more negative camber + 0.2mm extra toe-out to accelerate thermal buildup.

step6_dampers.hs_slope_reasoning: Explicit click/slope materialization from selected family settings.

constraints.name: Front LS damping ratio
constraints.note: Explicit click materialization recomputed from selected clicks.

constraints.name: Rear HS damping ratio
constraints.note: Explicit click materialization recomputed from selected clicks.

constraints.name: Front HS rbd > HS comp
constraints.note: Rebound must exceed comp to prevent wheel bounce.

constraints.name: Rear HS comp < Front HS comp
constraints.note: Compliance hierarchy: rear yields to bumps more than front.

notes: Front critical damping: 13652 N*s/m (modal k=172.3 N/mm, f_n = 4.02 Hz)
Rear critical damping: 13334 N*s/m (modal k=146.4 N/mm, f_n = 3.49 Hz)
Front LS: zeta=0.64 -> c=8757 N*s/m -> F@25mm/s = 219 N -> 11 clicks
Front HS: zeta=0.20 -> c=2756 N*s/m -> F@110mm/s = 303 N -> 7 clicks
Rear HS: zeta=0.25 -> c=3334 N*s/m -> F@107mm/s = 355 N -> 8 clicks
HS ref velocities: front p95=109.9mm/s, rear p95=106.6mm/s (rear -3% more active)
Damping ratios are derived from quarter-car eigenvalue analysis, NOT from empirical baseline matching.
Roll dampers: zeta_roll=0.55, C_crit_roll=26434 N*m*s/rad, front LS=7/HS=2/slope=11, rear=N/A (per-corner shocks)
Rear 3rd damper: zeta_ls=0.45, zeta_hs=0.25, C_crit=31559 N*s/m, LS comp/rbd=5/5, HS comp/rbd=5/5
Explicit damper materialization recomputed axle coefficients and damping ratios from selected clicks.

supporting.brake_bias_reasoning: Calibrated base: 44.8% | Fuel correction: -0.03% at 49L | Result: 44.7%; +0.8% from trail brake depth p95=1.00; +0.2% for high braking decel p95=2.09g; -0.5% for front braking lock proxy p95=0.109; ⚠ High brake deceleration asymmetry (30.8 m/s²) detected — check front caliper/pad condition before adjusting brake bias; -0.2% for front brake wheel decel asymmetry p95=30.8 m/s^2; Note: measured hydraulic split 75.6% vs recommended 45.0% (delta +30.6%); Current migration -1.0 retained as hardware context only; Current target -1.0 retained as hardware context only; MC: F/R = 20.6/22.2 mm (ratio 0.93); differs from nominal 1.00 — effective bias may need decrease; Pad: Medium — low brake temps, softer compound may improve initial bite | front-lock evidence shifted brake hardware and migration rearward
supporting.brake_hardware_status: Static brake bias is solved from telemetry; brake target, master cylinders, pad compound, and migration were conservatively seeded from braking evidence.
supporting.diff_reasoning: Base: 85.0 Nm (car default 85 Nm; lat transfer=15530N at peak lat_g=4.63g); +10 Nm for binary throttle (traction protection); -5 Nm for deep trail braking (rotation on coast); +5 Nm for rear power slip p95=0.104; clutch plates=4 | Coast 40 deg (deep trail braker, more entry lock = stability); Drive 65 deg (binary throttle, more exit lock = wheelspin protection); coast anchored to driver=40 deg; drive anchored to driver=65 deg | Lock: coast=63.2% drive=36.5% (preload 19.4% + plates 43.8%)
supporting.tc_reasoning: Baseline: gain=4, slip=3; +1 gain for erratic consistency; +1 slip for rear power slip p95=0.104; +1 gain for binary throttle; +1 gain: TC barely active (0%) but rear slip p95=0.104; +1 slip for high MGU-K torque 320 Nm; anchored to driver-loaded slip=6
supporting.pressure_reasoning: Track temp 38°C → cold adj -2.3 kPa; Front hot 170 kPa → cold 152 kPa; Rear hot 166 kPa → cold 152 kPa

warnings: Rear RH R²=0.99 vs Q²=0.76 (gap 0.22) — possible overfit
ARB has not been auto-validated. To upgrade to high confidence, run auto_calibrate with 3+ IBT sessions varying ARB sizes.
